fixed.market_context: AI synthetic user x design thinking x user research SaaS
fixed.product_category: B2B SaaS for AI pre-validation and research scoping
fixed.buyer_type: research owner, governance gatekeeper, and internal evaluator

preferred.location: Hong Kong enterprise or regional APAC corporate environment
preferred.company_stage: large enterprise with formal research, legal, and governance processes
preferred.work_context: supports multiple internal stakeholders, manages research demand, and constantly has to prioritize which questions deserve expensive human studies
preferred.research_maturity: high; methodologically aware and sensitive to misuse of weak evidence
current_workarounds: stakeholder education on what research can and cannot answer
product_focus: identifying high-risk assumptions before formal study
core_attraction: can help reduce wasted human-research cycles on poorly framed questions
can sharpen what should be tested with real participants
may improve internal stakeholder discipline before study kickoff
core_objections: may worry stakeholders will misuse synthetic output as evidence replacement
may reject the tool if auditability and governance are weak
may distrust the product if persona logic is not transparent enough for internal review
questions_to_surface: What guardrails stop this from replacing real user research in the wrong contexts?
How are persona provenance, prompt versions, and model details tracked?
Can this help me write better research questions rather than noisier ones?
What enterprise governance artifacts exist for review and audit?
preferred.trust_boundary: needs strong synthetic-only framing, visible audit trail, reproducibility hooks, and explicit enterprise-safe usage boundaries
preferred.pricing_sensitivity: lower than startup users if governance, reliability, and internal-control needs are met; much higher if the tool still feels experimental
preferred.retention_hypothesis: retains only if it becomes a trusted front-end triage layer that improves study quality without undermining research credibility
interview_focus: whether the product can be accepted as pre-research infrastructure
what governance and documentation are required for adoption
where the line between useful scoping and invalid substitution sits
